commands.repair_reason: - [P1] This needs maintainer product/security/API direction, not an automated repair, because the branch is a design-only PR for a new cross-channel delivery capability.; Needs attention: The patch has a low-severity redaction concern from a real-looking Telegram target and personal context in a public root document.; Review finding: [P2] Do not document unimplemented echo commands — CHANNEL-ECHO-RFC.md:13-25 - [P2]... commands.review_summary:
Adds a root RFC document proposing session-level echo targets, `openclaw sessions echo`, Gateway session RPC/schema additions, and outbound fanout across Telegram/Discord-style channels.

PR surface: Docs +167. Total +167 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is an RFC-only feature PR. Current source inspection confirms the proposed command, RPC, session field, and fanout path are not implemented on main.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This needs maintainer product/security/API direction, not an automated repair, because the branch is a design-only PR for a new cross-channel delivery capability.

target.title: RFC: Channel Echo / Session Pinning
target.body:
## Summary

Session-level echo channels: pin a session to one or more delivery targets (Telegram thread, Discord channel, etc.) so every turn — both user input and AI response — echoes to all pinned channels regardless of which transport originated the turn.

### Problem

When a session is used across multiple transports (e.g. Telegram + AR glasses + web UI), each transport only sees messages sent/received through it. Telegram loses visibility of exchanges that happened on the glasses. The AI has full context (session history is the source of truth), but the human-facing chat logs are fragmented across transports.

### Solution

- Add `echoChannels: Array<{channel, to, threadId?, accountId?}>` to session entries
- After primary delivery succeeds, fire best-effort copies to each pinned echo target
- Echo both the user message and AI response so pinned channels have the complete conversation
- Dedup: never echo back to the originating transport; `isEcho` flag prevents circular loops
- All echo deliveries are fire-and-forget — never block primary delivery

### User-facing API

```bash
# Pin a session to echo to a Telegram thread
openclaw sessions echo <sessionKey> --channel telegram --to 8661849123

# Remove an echo pin
openclaw sessions echo <sessionKey> --remove telegram:8661849123

# List echo pins
openclaw sessions echo <sessionKey> --list
```

### Implementation scope

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/config/sessions/types.ts` | `SessionEchoTarget` type + `echoChannels` field |
| `src/config/sessions/store.ts` | Read/write `echoChannels` in session load/save |
| `src/infra/outbound/echo.ts` (new) | Echo fanout: load targets, dedup, format, deliver |
| `src/infra/outbound/deliver.ts` | Post-delivery echo hook after `message:sent` |
| `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts` | Echo user messages from webchat/gateway path |
| `src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts` | `sessions.echo` method for add/remove/list |
| `src/gateway/protocol/schema/sessions.ts` | Schema for echo target CRUD |

### Motivation

Built for the VituReClaw project (OpenClaw-native Viture Pro Neckband app) — when wearing AR glasses and talking to OpenClaw, the conversation should also appear in the Telegram thread you normally use, so you have a single persistent log regardless of which device you're on.

Same pattern as IRC bouncers or Matrix bridging: session-level delivery fanout.

## Status

RFC only — design doc at `CHANNEL-ECHO-RFC.md`, no implementation code yet. ~11h estimated.

## Test plan

- [ ] Pin session to Telegram, send from glasses/webchat, verify echo appears in Telegram
- [ ] Send from Telegram on pinned session, verify no double-echo back to Telegram
- [ ] Pin to two targets, verify both receive echoes independently
- [ ] Remove a pin, verify echoes stop
- [ ] Verify primary delivery is never blocked by echo failures
- [ ] Verify circular echo protection (`isEcho` flag)
target.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia
